% Definition-16 fidelity audit sentence -- generated by compute_audit_stats.py
% Selected branch: primary (rate >= 90%). Numbers trace to audit_results.json.
% Placement: S7 setup paragraph, immediately after the same-family caveat.
A hand audit of 100 variants, sampled uniformly across the four benchmarks (seed = 42, at most one variant per assertion), found 95\% to instantiate the transformation types of Definition~\ref{def:varrel} without altering the proposition under evaluation; failing variants were left in place, so any residual infidelity inflates the measured migration rates --- a variant that shifts the proposition can legitimately fail verification --- adding a second reason, alongside the sampling noise bounded in \S\ref{sec:diagnostic}, to read the rates of Figure~\ref{fig:migration-tf-to-ff} as upper bounds. Per-edge fidelity validation at scale is part of the reliability program of \S\ref{sec:future-work}.

% --- other branch (not selected) ---
% A hand audit of 100 variants, sampled uniformly across the four benchmarks (seed = 42, at most one variant per assertion), found 95\% to instantiate the transformation types of Definition~\ref{def:varrel} without altering the proposition under evaluation; the failing 5\% were left in place and inflate the measured migration rates --- a variant that shifts the proposition can legitimately fail verification --- so the rates of Figure~\ref{fig:migration-tf-to-ff} are upper bounds on this count too, alongside the sampling noise bounded in \S\ref{sec:diagnostic}. Per-edge fidelity validation at scale is part of the reliability program of \S\ref{sec:future-work}.
